name: Security Audit
description: Full application security audit: map attack surface, test OWASP Top 10, check dependencies, review auth, and produce a prioritized findings report

steps.name: Map Attack Surface
steps.description: Enumerate all entry points: HTTP endpoints, CLI commands, file inputs, environment variables, third-party integrations, and data stores. Document what data flows where.
steps.acceptance_criteria: Complete attack surface map with all input vectors, data flows, and trust boundaries documented

steps.name: Authentication & Session Review
steps.description: Check auth implementation: password hashing algorithm, session token entropy, token expiry, refresh token rotation, logout behavior, and brute-force protection.
steps.acceptance_criteria: Auth mechanisms reviewed, any weak hashing/entropy/expiry issues documented with severity

steps.name: Injection & Input Validation
steps.description: Test for SQL injection, command injection, XSS, SSTI, path traversal, and XXE. Check every input that reaches a database, shell, template engine, or file system.
steps.acceptance_criteria: All input paths audited, injection vulnerabilities identified with reproduction steps

steps.name: Authorization & Access Control
steps.description: Verify that every protected resource checks authorization, not just authentication. Test for IDOR, privilege escalation, and missing object-level checks.
steps.acceptance_criteria: All authorization checks verified, IDOR risks and privilege escalation vectors identified

steps.name: Dependency Vulnerability Scan
steps.description: Run dependency audit tools (mix audit, npm audit, cargo audit, pip-audit). Check for known CVEs in all direct and transitive dependencies. Flag anything with CVSS >= 7.0.
steps.acceptance_criteria: Audit run, all high/critical CVEs listed with affected versions and available fixes

steps.name: Secrets & Configuration Audit
steps.description: Scan for hardcoded secrets, API keys, and credentials in source code, config files, and git history. Verify secrets are loaded from environment, not committed.
steps.acceptance_criteria: No secrets in source, all credentials via environment variables, git history scanned

steps.name: Findings Report
steps.description: Compile all findings into a prioritized security report. Categorize by CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW using CVSS scoring. Provide remediation steps for each finding.
steps.acceptance_criteria: Security report written with all findings, CVSS scores, and concrete remediation steps for each issue
